explicit_text: When I was about two and a half, my family moved to the United Arab Emirates because my parents were missionaries and church leaders there. We first lived in Al Ain, and later I spent my fourth through eighth grades in Abu Dhabi, which really felt like my hometown. I also returned to the UAE during college to work on a hospital project in Al Ain. The UAE was a big part of my childhood and early adulthood.

I also returned there during college to work on a hospital project in the oasis city. That federation was a big part of my childhood and early adulthood.

explicit_text: Al Ain was the first city we lived in when we moved to the United Arab Emirates in the mid-1960s. Later, during college, I returned to Al Ain to work on building a new hospital. Al Ain holds a special place in my memories of growing up abroad.

explicit_text: In 1967, we moved to Jordan for one year so my parents could study Arabic at a special language school. It was a brief but important time because it helped my parents improve their language skills for their missionary work. After that year, we returned to the UAE. Jordan was a key stop in our journey living in the Middle East.

explicit_text: I was born in Northern Wisconsin in June 1963 and lived there for the first two years of my life before moving abroad. Later, I returned to Wisconsin for ninth grade and then again for college at the University of Wisconsin–Madison. I've stayed in Madison for many years now, raising my family and watching my daughters grow up.
